finding: User has had to fix Kindle Scribe MTP access for Calibre on at least two separate occasions (Apr 3 and Apr 14). A udev rule at /etc/udev/rules.d/51-kindle-scribe.rules exists but does not reliably survive or take effect. The user escalated to Opus on the second occurrence after manually attempting the sudo fix. Goal: Kindle always works with Calibre on plug-in. Surface of recurrence: same device, same machine, across weeks.
existing_mitigation: A udev rule file exists at /etc/udev/rules.d/51-kindle-scribe.rules, but it does not appear to be captured in the NixOS config or dotfiles, making it fragile to OS changes.

finding: User manually asks whether Autodoro is running in at least 4 instances across nixos-config and autodoro project sessions. Autodoro is a break-timer with a systemd user unit, but there is no watchdog, auto-restart, or desktop notification on failure. When it silently stops, the user only notices after a long stretch without break prompts. Goal: Autodoro should always be running and the user should never need to check. Surface of recurrence: same service, across sessions, across time.
existing_mitigation: Autodoro has a systemd user service at ~/.config/systemd/user/autodoro.service, but it apparently lacks Restart=on-failure or a health-check mechanism.

finding: User asks Claude to git pull and build the artcraft project in 3 separate sessions spanning multiple weeks. Each time involves the same sequence: pull latest, resolve dependencies, build desktop app. The artcraft CLAUDE.md has project structure but no build shortcut or SessionStart automation. Goal: get artcraft to latest buildable state with minimal friction. Surface of recurrence: same project, across sessions, across weeks.
existing_mitigation: Artcraft has a detailed CLAUDE.md with project structure, but no build script or CLAUDE.md rule that auto-suggests building after pull.

finding: User is building a personal assistant to process voice transcriptions from phone (via Dropbox) into structured actions (TickTick tasks, project proposals). The core need -- recording thoughts on the go and having them automatically triaged into actionable items -- is currently fully manual. The user has specified MVP requirements including a cron-based agent, MCP-only actions, configurable human-in-the-loop, and security constraints. A spec exists at /home/jonathan/Repos/jonathans-assistant/docs/specs/. Goal: voice memos automatically become structured actions. Surface of recurrence: daily personal workflow, cross-device.
existing_mitigation: A spec exists and brainstorming session occurred, but no implementation yet. The repo is empty beyond docs.
